13: export class OrderFulfillmentService {
14:     private db: DatabaseClient;

48:     async getOrder(orderId: string): Promise<Order | null> {
49:         const row = await this.db.query('SELECT * FROM orders WHERE id = $1', [orderId]);
50:         if (!row) return null;
51:         return this.mapRowToOrder(row);

<- CUT CONTENT ->
60:     async fulfillOrder(orderId: string): Promise<{ invoice: Invoice; label: ShippingLabel }> {
61:         const order = await this.getOrder(orderId);
62: 
63:         const currentStock = await this.inventory.getStock(order.productId);
64:         const newStock = currentStock - order.quantity;
65:         await this.inventory.setStock(order.productId, newStock);
66: 
67:         const subtotal = order.quantity * order.unitPrice;
68:         const tax = subtotal * order.taxRate;
69:         const total = subtotal + tax;
70: 
71:         await this.payments.capture(order.paymentIntentId, subtotal);
72: 
73:         const invoice: Invoice = {
74:             orderId,
75:             amount: subtotal,
76:             tax,
77:             total,
78:             paidAt: new Date(),
79:         };
80:         await this.db.query(
81:             'INSERT INTO invoices (order_id, amount) VALUES ($1, $2)',
82:             [orderId, invoice.amount]
83:         );
84: 
85:         const label = await this.shipping.createLabel({
86:             orderId,
87:             address: order.shippingAddress,
88:             weight: order.weight,
89:         });
90: 
91:         await this.db.query(
92:             "UPDATE orders SET status = 'fulfilled' WHERE id = $1",
93:             [orderId]
94:         );
95: 
96:         return { invoice, label };
97:     }
<- CUT CONTENT ->
vars.prSummary: Add order fulfillment flow: check inventory, capture payment, generate invoice, create shipping label, and mark order fulfilled.

40:     async login(userId: string, existingSessionId?: string): Promise<Session> {
41:         const activeSessions = await this.db.query(
42:             'SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE user_id = $1 AND expires_at > $2',
43:             [userId, Date.now()]
44:         );
45: 
46:         if (activeSessions.length >= this.maxSessions) {
47:             const oldest = activeSessions.sort((a: Session, b: Session) => a.createdAt - b.createdAt)[0];
48:             await this.db.query('DELETE FROM sessions WHERE session_id = $1', [oldest.sessionId]);
49:         }
50: 
51:         const sessionId = existingSessionId || crypto.randomUUID();
52:         const token = crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex');
53:         const now = Date.now();
54: 
55:         await this.db.query(
56:             'INSERT INTO sessions (session_id, user_id, token, created_at, expires_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)',
57:             [sessionId, userId, token, now, now + this.sessionTtlMs]
58:         );
59: 
60:         return { sessionId, userId, token, createdAt: now, expiresAt: now + this.sessionTtlMs };
61:     }
62: 
63:     async validateSession(sessionId: string, providedToken: string): Promise<boolean> {
64:         const session = await this.db.query(
65:             'SELECT token, expires_at FROM sessions WHERE session_id = $1',
66:             [sessionId]
67:         );
68: 
69:         if (!session || session.expires_at < Date.now()) {
70:             return false;
71:         }
72: 
73:         return providedToken === session.token;
74:     }

vars.prSummary: Add session management: login with concurrent session limits, token-based session validation, and automatic eviction of oldest sessions.

    "suggestionContent": "Timing attack on session token comparison. Line 73 uses `===` to compare `providedToken` with `session.token`. The strict equality operator short-circuits on the first byte difference, meaning response time leaks how many leading characters are correct. An attacker can brute-force the 64-character hex token one character at a time (16 possibilities per position = ~1024 requests instead of 16^64). Should use `crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(providedToken), Buffer.from(session.token))` for constant-time comparison.",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Timing attack -- uses === for token comparison instead of constant-time crypto.timingSafeEqual, allowing character-by-character brute force"
  },

    "label": "security",
    "severity": "high",
    "language": "TypeScript",
    "existingCode": "        const sessionId = existingSessionId || crypto.randomUUID();",
    "improvedCode": "        const sessionId = crypto.randomUUID();",
    "relevantFile": "services/auth/UserSessionManager.ts",
    "relevantLinesStart": 51,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 51,
    "suggestionContent": "Session fixation vulnerability. Line 51 accepts an `existingSessionId` parameter and reuses it instead of always generating a fresh ID. An attacker can fixate a session by providing a known session ID: (1) attacker crafts a login link with `existingSessionId='attacker-known-id'`, (2) victim clicks and logs in, (3) the server creates a valid session with the attacker's chosen ID, (4) the attacker can now use 'attacker-known-id' to access the victim's session. The `login` method should always generate a new session ID via `crypto.randomUUID()` and ignore any client-supplied value.",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Session fixation -- accepts caller-supplied session ID instead of always generating a new one, allowing attackers to pre-set the session ID"
  },
  {
    "label": "bug",
    "severity": "medium",
    "language": "TypeScript",
    "existingCode": "        if (activeSessions.length >= this.maxSessions) {\n            const oldest = activeSessions.sort((a: Session, b: Session) => a.createdAt - b.createdAt)[0];\n            await this.db.query('DELETE FROM sessions WHERE session_id = $1', [oldest.sessionId]);\n        }",
    "improvedCode": "        if (activeSessions.length >= this.maxSessions) {\n            const sorted = activeSessions.sort((a: Session, b: Session) => a.createdAt - b.createdAt);\n            const toEvict = sorted.slice(0, activeSessions.length - this.maxSessions + 1);\n            for (const s of toEvict) {\n                await this.db.query('DELETE FROM sessions WHERE session_id = $1', [s.sessionId]);\n            }\n        }",
    "relevantFile": "services/auth/UserSessionManager.ts",
    "relevantLinesStart": 46,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 49,
    "suggestionContent": "Off-by-one in session limit allows maxSessions+1 concurrent sessions. Line 46 checks `activeSessions.length >= this.maxSessions` and if true, deletes the oldest session. But then lines 55-58 unconditionally create a new session. When `activeSessions.length === maxSessions`, one is deleted and one is added, maintaining the count at maxSessions -- this is correct. But when `activeSessions.length === maxSessions - 1`, no deletion happens and one is added, reaching maxSessions -- also correct. The actual problem: the condition should be `>=` with deletion of enough sessions. With `maxSessions = 3` and 3 active sessions: it deletes 1 (leaving 2), then adds 1 (now 3). But if two concurrent login calls both read 3 active sessions, both delete 1 each (leaving 1), then both add 1 each (now 3). The real issue is simpler: `>=` should be `>` because when length equals maxSessions, we already have the maximum. Deleting one then adding one is a no-op on count. When length is exactly maxSessions and we use `>=`, we delete+add keeping it at max. But consider: maxSessions=3, currently 3 sessions. We want to allow login so we evict oldest. The condition works. Actually the off-by-one: with `>=`, when length = maxSessions (e.g., 3), it evicts 1 then creates 1. Final count = 3. When length = maxSessions-1 (e.g., 2), no eviction, creates 1. Final count = 3. When length = maxSessions+1 (shouldn't happen but race). The real bug: the check evicts only 1 session but should evict enough to get below limit. If somehow there are maxSessions+2 due to races, it only evicts 1, resulting in maxSessions+2 sessions after the add.",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Off-by-one -- only evicts one session regardless of how many over the limit, and concurrent logins can bypass the limit entirely"
  }
]

30:     async processBatch(batchId: string): Promise<{ succeeded: number; failed: number }> {
31:         const subscriptionIds: string[] = await this.db.query(
32:             'SELECT id FROM subscriptions WHERE status = $1 AND next_billing_date <= $2',
33:             ['active', new Date()]
34:         );
35: 
36:         let succeeded = 0;
37:         let failed = 0;
38: 
39:         for (const subId of subscriptionIds) {
40:             const subscription: Subscription = await this.db.query(
41:                 'SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE id = $1',
42:                 [subId]
43:             );
44: 
45:             const amount = subscription.price * subscription.quantity;
46:             const platformFee = amount * 0.029 + 0.30;
47:             const totalCharge = amount + platformFee;
48: 
49:             try {
50:                 await this.paymentGateway.charge(subscription.paymentMethodId, totalCharge);
51:             } catch (err) {
52:                 this.logger.error(`Payment failed for subscription ${subId}: ${err.message}`);
53:             }
54: 
55:             succeeded++;
56: 
57:             await this.db.query(
58:                 'UPDATE subscriptions SET next_billing_date = $1, last_payment_amount = $2 WHERE id = $3',
59:                 [this.getNextBillingDate(subscription), totalCharge, subId]
60:             );
61:         }
62: 
63:         this.logger.info(`Batch ${batchId}: ${succeeded} succeeded, ${failed} failed`);
64:         return { succeeded, failed };
65:     }
<- CUT CONTENT ->
vars.prSummary: Add batch payment processing for monthly subscription billing. Iterates over due subscriptions, charges each, and updates the next billing date.

5: import Redis from 'ioredis';
6: 
7: export class CacheManager {
8:     private localCache: Map<string, { value: any; expiresAt: number }>;
9:     private redis: Redis;
10:     private db: DatabaseClient;
11:     private logger: Logger;
12: 
13:     constructor(redis: Redis, db: DatabaseClient, logger: Logger) {
14:         this.localCache = new Map();
15:         this.redis = redis;
16:         this.db = db;
17:         this.logger = logger;
18:     }
<- CUT CONTENT ->
30:     async get<T>(key: string, fetchFn: () => Promise<T>, ttlMs: number = 60000): Promise<T> {
31:         const localEntry = this.localCache.get(key);
32:         if (localEntry && localEntry.expiresAt > Date.now()) {
33:             return localEntry.value as T;
34:         }
35: 
36:         const redisValue = await this.redis.get(key);
37:         if (redisValue) {
38:             const parsed = JSON.parse(redisValue) as T;
39:             this.localCache.set(key, { value: parsed, expiresAt: Date.now() + ttlMs });
40:             return parsed;
41:         }
42: 
43:         const freshValue = await fetchFn();
44:         await this.redis.set(key, JSON.stringify(freshValue), 'PX', ttlMs);
45:         this.localCache.set(key, { value: freshValue, expiresAt: Date.now() + ttlMs });
46:         return freshValue;
47:     }
48: 
49:     async invalidate(key: string): Promise<void> {
50:         await this.redis.del(key);
51:         this.logger.info(`Cache invalidated: ${key}`);
52:     }
53: 
54:     deepMerge(target: Record<string, any>, source: Record<string, any>): Record<string, any> {
55:         const result = { ...target };
56:         for (const key of Object.keys(source)) {
57:             if (typeof source[key] === 'object' && source[key] !== null && typeof result[key] === 'object') {
58:                 result[key] = this.deepMerge(result[key], source[key]);
59:             } else {
60:                 result[key] = source[key];
61:             }
62:         }
63:         return result;
64:     }
65: 
66:     async getConfig(configKey: string): Promise<Record<string, any>> {
67:         const defaults = await this.get(`config:defaults:${configKey}`, () =>
68:             this.db.query('SELECT value FROM config WHERE key = $1', [configKey])
69:         );
70:         const overrides = await this.get(`config:overrides:${configKey}`, () =>
71:             this.db.query('SELECT value FROM config_overrides WHERE key = $1', [configKey])
72:         );
73:         return this.deepMerge(defaults, overrides);
74:     }
<- CUT CONTENT ->
vars.prSummary: Add multi-level cache with local Map + Redis, TTL-based expiration, cache invalidation, deep-merge config loading, and getConfig helper.

14: export class WebhookDispatcher {
15:     private db: DatabaseClient;

35:     async dispatch(event: string, payload: Record<string, any>): Promise<void> {
36:         const webhooks: WebhookConfig[] = await this.db.query(
37:             'SELECT url, secret, events FROM webhooks WHERE active = true',
38:             []
39:         );
40: 
41:         const relevant = webhooks.filter(w => w.events.includes(event));
42: 
43:         for (const webhook of relevant) {
44:             const signature = crypto
45:                 .createHmac('sha256', this.signingKey)
46:                 .update(JSON.stringify(payload))
47:                 .digest('hex');
48: 
49:             await this.sendWithRetry(webhook.url, payload, signature);
50:         }
51:     }
52: 
53:     private async sendWithRetry(url: string, payload: Record<string, any>, signature: string): Promise<void> {
54:         let attempt = 0;
55: 
56:         while (true) {
57:             attempt++;
58:             try {
59:                 await axios.post(url, payload, {
60:                     headers: {
61:                         'Content-Type': 'application/json',
62:                         'X-Webhook-Signature': signature,
63:                     },
64:                     timeout: 10000,
65:                 });
66:                 return;
67:             } catch (err: any) {
68:                 if (err.response && err.response.status >= 500) {
69:                     this.logger.warn(`Webhook delivery to ${url} failed (attempt ${attempt}), retrying...`);
70:                     await this.sleep(1000 * attempt);
71:                 } else {
72:                     throw err;
73:                 }
74:             }
75:         }
76:     }
77: 
78:     private sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
79:         return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
80:     }
<- CUT CONTENT ->

30:     async isAllowed(request: { userId?: string; ip: string; headers: Record<string, string> }): Promise<{ allowed: boolean; remaining: number }> {
31:         const clientIp = request.headers['x-forwarded-for'] || request.ip;
32:         const key = request.userId
33:             ? `rate:user:${request.userId}`
34:             : `rate:ip:${clientIp}`;
35: 
36:         const windowKey = Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000);
37:         const fullKey = `${key}:${windowKey}`;
38: 
39:         const currentCount = await this.redis.get(fullKey);
40:         const count = parseInt(currentCount || '0', 10);
41: 
42:         if (count >= this.config.maxRequests) {
43:             this.logger.warn(`Rate limit exceeded for ${key}`);
44:             return { allowed: false, remaining: 0 };
45:         }
46: 
47:         await this.redis.incr(fullKey);
48:         await this.redis.pexpire(fullKey, this.config.windowMs);
49: 
50:         return { allowed: true, remaining: this.config.maxRequests - count - 1 };
51:     }

5: interface MigrationConfig {
6:     sourceTable: string;
7:     batchSize: number;
8:     transformFn: (record: Record<string, any>) => Record<string, any>;
9: }
10: 
11: export class DataMigrationRunner {
12:     private db: DatabaseClient;

<- CUT CONTENT ->
30:     async runMigration(config: MigrationConfig): Promise<{ migrated: number; skipped: number }> {
31:         const { sourceTable, batchSize, transformFn } = config;
32:         let offset = batchSize;
33:         let migrated = 0;
34:         let skipped = 0;
35: 
36:         while (true) {
37:             const batch = await this.db.query(
38:                 `SELECT * FROM ${sourceTable} ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2`,
39:                 [batchSize, offset]
40:             );
41: 
42:             if (batch.length === 0) break;
43: 
44:             for (const record of batch) {
45:                 if (record.status == 0) {
46:                     skipped++;
47:                     continue;
48:                 }
49: 
50:                 const transformed = transformFn(record);
51: 
52:                 await this.db.query(
53:                     `UPDATE ${sourceTable} SET data = $1, version = $2, migrated_at = $3 WHERE id = $4`,
54:                     [JSON.stringify(transformed.data), transformed.version, new Date(), record.id]
55:                 );
56: 
57:                 migrated++;
58:             }
59: 
60:             offset += batchSize;
61:         }
62: 
63:         this.logger.info(`Migration complete: ${migrated} migrated, ${skipped} skipped`);
64:         return { migrated, skipped };
65:     }
<- CUT CONTENT ->

If the process crashes mid-migration (e.g., after processing 3 of 10 batches), the first 3 batches are permanently altered while the remaining 7 are untouched. Concrete exploit: `search(\"' UNION SELECT id, email, password, 0 FROM users --\", 'products')` returns all user credentials in the search results. The `to_tsquery($1)` at line 32 shows the developer knows about parameterized queries but failed to use them for the WHERE clause. Must use $2 and $3 parameters for `index` and `query`.",

    "relevantFile": "services/search/SearchIndexer.ts",
    "relevantLinesStart": 31,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 37,
    "suggestionContent": "Unbounded result set. The SQL query at lines 31-37 has no LIMIT clause. A broad search term like a single common word could match millions of rows in a large index, all loaded into memory at once. This causes out-of-memory crashes and extreme response times. Concrete scenario: `search('the', 'articles')` on an index of 10 million articles matches 8 million rows. Node.js attempts to allocate memory for 8 million objects, exceeding the V8 heap limit and crashing with FATAL ERROR: CALL_AND_RETRY_LAST Allocation failed. But `rows.length === 0` means the map callback never executes, so this specific case is safe. More critically, `totalDocuments` is the result of a COUNT query, and `totalDocuments.count` could be 0 if the index is empty or was just created. React uses keys to match old and new virtual DOM nodes. When the user sorts from ascending to descending, row 0 changes from 'Apple' to 'Zebra', but React thinks it is the same element (key=0) and reuses the DOM node, potentially preserving stale input states, focus, or animation state from the old row. If onRowClick handlers are attached, clicking after a sort may dispatch the wrong row data. Should use a stable, unique identifier: `key={row.id ?? String(row[columns[0].key])}`.", "oneSentenceSummary": "Using array index as key causes incorrect DOM recycling when rows are reordered by sorting or filtered" }, { "label": "security", "severity": "critical", "language": "TypeScript", "existingCode": " : ", "improvedCode": " : String(row[col.key])", "relevantFile": "src/components/DataTable.tsx", "relevantLinesStart": 86, "relevantLinesEnd": 86, "suggestionContent": "Line 86 renders cell values using dangerouslySetInnerHTML without any sanitization. When a column does not have a custom render function, the fallback path converts the cell value to a string and renders it as raw HTML. If any row data comes from user input (e.g., a user's name field, a product description, a comment), an attacker can inject malicious content such as '' which executes JavaScript in every user's browser viewing the table. The dangerouslySetInnerHTML should be removed entirely; plain text rendering with React's default escaping is sufficient here.", "oneSentenceSummary": "XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML renders user-controlled cell data as raw HTML without sanitization" } ]

vars.prSummary: Add real-time ChatWidget component with WebSocket connection, message history, typing indicators, and inline delete functionality.
vars.referenceCodeSuggestions:
[
  {
    "label": "bug",

    "language": "TypeScript",
    "existingCode": "  useEffect(() => {\n    const ws = new WebSocket(`${wsUrl}/rooms/${roomId}`);\n    wsRef.current = ws;\n\n    ws.onopen = () => {\n      setIsConnected(true);\n    };\n\n    ws.onmessage = (event) => {\n      const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n      if (data.type === 'message') {\n        setMessages([...messages, data.payload]);\n      } else if (data.type === 'typing') {\n        setTypingUsers(data.users);\n      }\n    };\n\n    ws.onclose = () => {\n      setIsConnected(false);\n    };\n\n    ws.onerror = () => {\n      setIsConnected(false);\n    };\n  }, [roomId, wsUrl]);",
    "improvedCode": "  useEffect(() => {\n    const ws = new WebSocket(`${wsUrl}/rooms/${roomId}`);\n    wsRef.current = ws;\n\n    ws.onopen = () => setIsConnected(true);\n\n    ws.onmessage = (event) => {\n      const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n      if (data.type === 'message') {\n        setMessages(prev => [...prev, data.payload]);\n      } else if (data.type === 'typing') {\n        setTypingUsers(data.users);\n      }\n    };\n\n    ws.onclose = () => setIsConnected(false);\n    ws.onerror = () => setIsConnected(false);\n\n    return () => ws.close();\n  }, [roomId, wsUrl]);",
    "relevantFile": "src/components/ChatWidget.tsx",
    "relevantLinesStart": 44,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 68,
    "suggestionContent": "The useEffect on lines 44-68 creates a new WebSocket connection but does not return a cleanup function that calls ws.close(). When the component unmounts (e.g., user navigates to a different page) or when roomId/wsUrl changes triggering a re-run of the effect, the old WebSocket remains open. Each re-render of the effect creates an additional connection without closing the previous one. Over time this leaks WebSocket connections, consuming server resources and bandwidth. The orphaned connections also continue to fire onmessage handlers that call setMessages and setIsConnected on a potentially unmounted component. Must add `return () => ws.close();` as the cleanup function.",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "WebSocket connection not closed on unmount or dependency change leaks connections and causes state updates on unmounted component"
  },
  {
    "label": "bug",
    "severity": "critical",
    "language": "TypeScript",
    "existingCode": "    setMessages([...messages, message]);\n    setInputText('');",
    "improvedCode": "    setMessages(prev => [...prev, message]);\n    setInputText('');",
    "relevantFile": "src/components/ChatWidget.tsx",
    "relevantLinesStart": 74,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 92,
    "suggestionContent": "The sendMessage function on lines 74-92 is an async function that calls setMessages and setInputText after sending via WebSocket. If the component unmounts between the ws.send() call and the state updates (e.g., user navigates away immediately after clicking Send), the setState calls target an unmounted component. Additionally, the function captures `messages` and `inputText` from the closure at render time. If the user types and sends multiple messages rapidly, each sendMessage invocation has a stale copy of messages from its closure, so the setMessages call on line 90 uses spread with the old array, potentially dropping messages that were added by concurrent sendMessage calls or incoming WebSocket messages. Should use functional updater: setMessages(prev => [...prev, message]) and add a mounted ref guard.",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Race condition in sendMessage due to stale closure over messages array can drop concurrent messages"
  },
  {
    "label": "bug",
    "severity": "critical",
    "language": "TypeScript",
    "existingCode": "      if (data.type === 'message') {\n        setMessages([...messages, data.payload]);",
    "improvedCode": "      if (data.type === 'message') {\n        setMessages(prev => [...prev, data.payload]);",
    "relevantFile": "src/components/ChatWidget.tsx",
    "relevantLinesStart": 52,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 58,
    "suggestionContent": "The ws.onmessage handler on lines 52-58 uses `setMessages([...messages, data.payload])` which captures the `messages` array from the closure at the time the effect ran (initially an empty array). Because the effect has [roomId, wsUrl] as dependencies and does not re-run when messages change, the handler always spreads the stale initial messages array. When message A arrives, it correctly produces [A]. But when message B arrives, the handler still sees the original empty array and produces [B] instead of [A, B], effectively overwriting message A. This is a classic stale closure bug. Must use the functional updater form: `setMessages(prev => [...prev, data.payload])`.",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Stale closure in ws.onmessage overwrites messages because handler captures initial empty messages array"
  },
  {
    "label": "performance",
    "severity": "critical",
    "language": "TypeScript",
    "existingCode": "      <MessageList messages={messages} onDelete={(id) => handleDelete(id)} />",
    "improvedCode": "      <MessageList messages={messages} onDelete={handleDelete} />",
    "relevantFile": "src/components/ChatWidget.tsx",
    "relevantLinesStart": 113,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 113,
    "suggestionContent": "On line 113, the onDelete prop passed to MessageList is an inline arrow function: `onDelete={(id) => handleDelete(id)}`. Since MessageList is wrapped in React.memo (line 22), it should only re-render when its props change. However, this inline arrow function creates a new function reference on every render of ChatWidget, causing React.memo's shallow comparison to always detect a prop change, defeating the memoization entirely. Every time ChatWidget re-renders (e.g., typing in the input, receiving a typing indicator), MessageList re-renders the entire message list. With hundreds of messages, this causes noticeable jank. Should pass a stable reference: `onDelete={handleDelete}` (and wrap handleDelete in useCallback).",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Inline arrow function as onDelete prop defeats React.memo on MessageList causing unnecessary re-renders"
  }
]

vars.prSummary: Add drag-and-drop FileUploader component with progress bar, image preview, and client-side file type validation.
Add DashboardLayout component with sidebar navigation, breadcrumbs, responsive behavior, and redirect handling for the authenticated app shell.
Add SearchAutocomplete component with debounced API search, keyboard navigation, and dropdown suggestions list.
Add multi-step FormWizard component with per-step validation, draft saving, and step navigation for onboarding flow.
Add NotificationCenter component with Server-Sent Events for real-time notifications, toast display, and mark-as-read functionality.
Add MediaPlayer component with audio playback controls, progress tracking, volume control, and interactive playlist.
Add ThemeProvider with dark/light mode toggle, system preference detection, custom theme overrides, and localStorage persistence.

12: class DataPipeline:
13:     """ETL pipeline that reads from multiple sources, transforms, and loads to DB."""
14: 
15:     def __init__(self, db_client, config: Dict[str, Any]):
16:         self.db = db_client
17:         self.config = config
18:         self.batch_size = config.get('batch_size', 1000)
19: 
20:     def run_pipeline(self, sources: list = []) -> Dict[str, Any]:
21:         """Execute the full ETL pipeline across given sources."""
22:         results = {'processed': 0, 'errors': 0, 'loaded': 0}
23:         sources.append(self.config.get('default_source', '/data/default.json'))
24:         for source_path in sources:
25:             raw_data = self._extract(source_path)
26:             transformed = self._transform(raw_data)
27:             self._load(transformed)
28:             results['processed'] += len(raw_data)
29:             results['loaded'] += len(transformed)
30:         return results

32:     def _extract(self, filepath: str) -> List[Dict]:
33:         """Read data from a JSON source file."""
34:         logger.info(f"Extracting data from {filepath}")
35:         data = open(filepath).read()
36:         records = json.loads(data)
37:         logger.info(f"Extracted {len(records)} records")
38:         return records
39: 
40:     def _transform(self, records: List[Dict]) -> List[Dict]:
41:         """Apply transformation rules to records."""
42:         transformed = []
43:         for record in records:
44:             try:
45:                 expr = self.config['transform_expression']
46:                 transform_fn = eval(expr)
47:                 result = transform_fn(record)
48:                 transformed.append(result)
49:             except:
50:                 logger.warning("skipping record")
51:         return transformed
52: 
53:     def _load(self, records: List[Dict]) -> None:
54:         """Load transformed records into the database in batches."""
55:         for i in range(0, len(records), self.batch_size):
56:             batch = records[i:i + self.batch_size]
57:             self.db.bulk_insert('pipeline_output', batch)
58:             logger.info(f"Loaded batch of {len(batch)} records")
59: 
60:     def _validate_record(self, record: Dict) -> Dict:
61:         """Validate a single record before transformation."""
62:         if 'id' not in record:
63:             raise ValueError("Record missing required field 'id'")
64:         if 'timestamp' not in record:
65:             raise ValueError("Record missing required field 'timestamp'")
66:         return record
67: 
68:     def load_config(self, config_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
69:         """Load pipeline configuration from a YAML file."""
70:         with open(config_path, 'r') as f:
71:             return yaml.safe_load(f)
72: 
73:     def export_results(self, results: List[Dict], output_path: str) -> None:
74:         """Export pipeline results to a JSONL file."""
75:         with open(output_path, 'w') as f:
76:             for result in results:

81:         """Return the current pipeline status."""

83:             'batch_size': self.batch_size,
84:             'db_connected': self.db.is_connected(),

16 +        self.db = db_client
17 +        self.config = config
18 +        self.batch_size = config.get('batch_size', 1000)
19 +
20 +    def run_pipeline(self, sources: list = []) -> Dict[str, Any]:
21 +        """Execute the full ETL pipeline across given sources."""
22 +        results = {'processed': 0, 'errors': 0, 'loaded': 0}
23 +        sources.append(self.config.get('default_source', '/data/default.json'))
24 +        for source_path in sources:
25 +            raw_data = self._extract(source_path)

36 +        records = json.loads(data)
37 +        logger.info(f"Extracted {len(records)} records")
38 +        return records
39 +
40 +    def _transform(self, records: List[Dict]) -> List[Dict]:
41 +        """Apply transformation rules to records."""
42 +        transformed = []
43 +        for record in records:
44 +            try:
45 +                expr = self.config['transform_expression']
46 +                transform_fn = eval(expr)
47 +                result = transform_fn(record)
48 +                transformed.append(result)
49 +            except:
50 +                logger.warning("skipping record")
51 +        return transformed
52 +
53 +    def _load(self, records: List[Dict]) -> None:

10 +class UserService:
11 +    """User management with registration, profile updates, and password handling."""

13 +    ALLOWED_PROFILE_FIELDS = ['username', 'email', 'bio', 'avatar_url']
14 +
15 +    def __init__(self, db_client):
16 +        self.db = db_client
17 +        self.logger = logger
18 +
19 +    def register_user(self, username: str, email: str, password: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
20 +        """Register a new user account."""
21 +        self.logger.info(f"Registering user: {username}")
22 +        password_hash = hashlib.md5(password.encode()).hexdigest()
23 +        user = {
24 +            'username': username,
25 +            'email': email,
26 +            'password_hash': password_hash,
27 +            'created_at': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
28 +            'is_admin': False,
29 +            'is_active': True,
30 +        }
31 +        self.db.users.insert_one(user)
32 +        self.logger.info(f"User {username} registered successfully")
33 +        return user

35 +    def authenticate(self, email: str, password: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:

37 +        user = self.db.users.find_one({'email': email})
38 +        if not user:
39 +            return None
40 +        input_hash = hashlib.md5(password.encode()).hexdigest()
41 +        if user['password_hash'] == input_hash:
42 +            return user
43 +        return None
44 +
45 +    def get_user_role(self, user: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
46 +        """Determine the user's role based on their attributes."""

50 +            return 'admin'

52 +            return 'moderator'

2: import hmac
3: import hashlib
4: import time
5: import logging
6: import requests
7: from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
8: 
9: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
10: 
11: 
12: class ApiClient:
13:     """HTTP API client with retry logic, authentication, and request signing."""
14: 
15:     def __init__(self, base_url: str, api_key: str, api_secret: str, max_retries: int = 3):
16:         self.base_url = base_url.rstrip('/')
17:         self.api_key = api_key
18:         self.api_secret = api_secret
19:         self.max_retries = max_retries
20:         self.timeout = 30.0
21: 
22:     def get(self, endpoint: str, params: Optional[Dict] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
23:         """Perform a signed GET request with retry."""
24:         url = f"{self.base_url}/{endpoint.lstrip('/')}"
25:         return self._request_with_retry('GET', url, params=params)
26: 
27:     def post(self, endpoint: str, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
28:         """Perform a signed POST request with retry."""
29:         url = f"{self.base_url}/{endpoint.lstrip('/')}"
30:         return self._request_with_retry('POST', url, json_data=data)
31: 
32:     def _sign_request(self, method: str, url: str, timestamp: str) -> str:
33:         """Generate HMAC signature for request authentication."""
34:         message = f"{method}:{url}:{timestamp}"
35:         logger.debug(f"Request signed with key={self.api_key} secret={self.api_secret}")
36:         signature = hmac.new(
37:             self.api_secret.encode(),
38:             message.encode(),
39:             hashlib.sha256
40:         ).hexdigest()
41:         return signature
42: 
43:     def _request_with_retry(self, method: str, url: str,
44:                              params: Optional[Dict] = None,
45:                              json_data: Optional[Dict] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
46:         """Execute HTTP request with retry on failure."""
47:         timestamp = str(int(time.time()))
48:         signature = self._sign_request(method, url, timestamp)
49:         headers = {
50:             'X-API-Key': self.api_key,
51:             'X-Timestamp': timestamp,
52:             'X-Signature': signature,
53:         }
54: 
55:         for i in range(self.max_retries):
56:             try:
57:                 if method == 'GET':
58:                     response = requests.get(url, params=params, headers=headers,
59:                                             timeout=self.timeout)
60:                 elif method == 'POST':
61:                     response = requests.post(url, json=json_data, headers=headers,
62:                                              timeout=self.timeout)
63:                 else:
64:                     raise ValueError(f"Unsupported method: {method}")
65: 
66:                 if response.elapsed.total_seconds() == 30.0:
67:                     logger.warning("Request timed out")
68: 
69:                 response.raise_for_status()
70:                 return response.json()
71: 
72:             except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
73:                 logger.warning(f"Request attempt {i + 1} failed: {e}")
74:                 continue
75: 
76:     def health_check(self) -> bool:
77:         """Check if the API is reachable."""
78:         try:
79:             response = requests.get(f"{self.base_url}/health", timeout=5)
80:             return response.status_code == 200
81:         except requests.ConnectionError:
82:             return False

12 +class ApiClient:
13 +    """HTTP API client with retry logic, authentication, and request signing."""
14 +
15 +    def __init__(self, base_url: str, api_key: str, api_secret: str, max_retries: int = 3):
16 +        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip('/')
17 +        self.api_key = api_key
18 +        self.api_secret = api_secret
19 +        self.max_retries = max_retries
20 +        self.timeout = 30.0
21 +
22 +    def get(self, endpoint: str, params: Optional[Dict] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
23 +        """Perform a signed GET request with retry."""
24 +        url = f"{self.base_url}/{endpoint.lstrip('/')}"
25 +        return self._request_with_retry('GET', url, params=params)
26 +
27 +    def post(self, endpoint: str, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
28 +        """Perform a signed POST request with retry."""
29 +        url = f"{self.base_url}/{endpoint.lstrip('/')}"
30 +        return self._request_with_retry('POST', url, json_data=data)
31 +
32 +    def _sign_request(self, method: str, url: str, timestamp: str) -> str:
33 +        """Generate HMAC signature for request authentication."""
34 +        message = f"{method}:{url}:{timestamp}"
35 +        logger.debug(f"Request signed with key={self.api_key} secret={self.api_secret}")
36 +        signature = hmac.new(
37 +            self.api_secret.encode(),
38 +            message.encode(),
39 +            hashlib.sha256
40 +        ).hexdigest()
41 +        return signature
42 +
43 +    def _request_with_retry(self, method: str, url: str,
44 +                             params: Optional[Dict] = None,
45 +                             json_data: Optional[Dict] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
46 +        """Execute HTTP request with retry on failure."""
47 +        timestamp = str(int(time.time()))
48 +        signature = self._sign_request(method, url, timestamp)
49 +        headers = {
50 +            'X-API-Key': self.api_key,
51 +            'X-Timestamp': timestamp,
52 +            'X-Signature': signature,
53 +        }
54 +
55 +        for i in range(self.max_retries):
56 +            try:
57 +                if method == 'GET':
58 +                    response = requests.get(url, params=params, headers=headers,
59 +                                            timeout=self.timeout)
60 +                elif method == 'POST':
61 +                    response = requests.post(url, json=json_data, headers=headers,
62 +                                             timeout=self.timeout)
63 +                else:
64 +                    raise ValueError(f"Unsupported method: {method}")
65 +
66 +                if response.elapsed.total_seconds() == 30.0:
67 +                    logger.warning("Request timed out")
68 +
69 +                response.raise_for_status()
70 +                return response.json()
71 +
72 +            except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
73 +                logger.warning(f"Request attempt {i + 1} failed: {e}")
74 +                continue
75 +
76 +    def health_check(self) -> bool:
77 +        """Check if the API is reachable."""
78 +        try:
79 +            response = requests.get(f"{self.base_url}/health", timeout=5)
80 +            return response.status_code == 200
81 +        except requests.ConnectionError:
82 +            return False
83 +
84 +    def close(self) -> None:
85 +        """Cleanup client resources."""
86 +        logger.info("ApiClient closed")

11 +class FileProcessor:
12 +    """Processes uploaded files: validates, transforms, and stores them."""
13 +
14 +    ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS = {'.txt', '.csv', '.json', '.xml', '.log'}
15 +    MAX_FILE_SIZE = 50 * 1024 * 1024  # 50MB
16 +
17 +    def __init__(self, upload_dir: str, storage_dir: str):
18 +        self.upload_dir = upload_dir
19 +        self.storage_dir = storage_dir
20 +
21 +    def validate_extension(self, filename: str) -> bool:
22 +        """Check if file extension is allowed."""
23 +        _, ext = os.path.splitext(filename)
24 +        return ext.lower() in self.ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS
25 +
26 +    def resolve_upload_path(self, filename: str) -> str:
27 +        """Build full path for an uploaded file."""
28 +        filepath = os.path.join(self.upload_dir, filename)
29 +        return filepath
30 +
31 +    def process_file(self, filename: str) -> Dict:
32 +        """Process an uploaded file: validate, read, transform, store."""
33 +        if not self.validate_extension(filename):
34 +            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported file type: {filename}")
35 +
36 +        filepath = self.resolve_upload_path(filename)
37 +
38 +        if os.path.exists(filepath):
39 +            data = open(filepath).read()
40 +        else:
41 +            raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {filepath}")
42 +
43 +        file_hash = hashlib.md5(data.encode()).hexdigest()
44 +        transformed = self._transform(data)
45 +
46 +        dest_path = os.path.join(self.storage_dir, f"{file_hash}_{filename}")
47 +        with open(dest_path, 'w') as out:
48 +            out.write(transformed)
49 +
50 +        logger.info(f"Processed {filename} -> {dest_path}")
51 +        return {
52 +            'original': filename,
53 +            'hash': file_hash,
54 +            'dest': dest_path,
55 +            'size': len(transformed),
56 +        }
57 +
58 +    def _transform(self, content: str) -> str:
59 +        """Apply transformations to file content."""
60 +        lines = content.splitlines()
61 +        cleaned = [line.strip() for line in lines if line.strip()]
62 +        return "\n".join(cleaned)
63 +
64 +    def get_file_info(self, filename: str) -> Dict:
65 +        """Get file metadata without processing."""

68 +            raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {filepath}")

72 +            'name': filename,

75 +        }

78 +        """Count lines in a file."""

61:         """Send SMS asynchronously."""

3: import json
4: import time
5: import logging
6: from typing import Dict, Callable, Optional, Any
7: 
8: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
9:

11: class QueueWorker:
12:     """Processes messages from a queue with retry and dead-letter."""
13: 
14:     def __init__(self, channel, queue_name: str, handler: Callable,
15:                  max_retries: int = 3, retry_delay: float = 5.0):
16:         self.channel = channel
17:         self.queue_name = queue_name
18:         self.handler = handler
19:         self.max_retries = max_retries
20:         self.retry_delay = retry_delay
21:         self.dead_letter_queue: str = f"{queue_name}.dlq"
22:         self.running = False
23:         self.stats = {'processed': 0, 'failed': 0, 'retried': 0}
24: 
25:     def start(self) -> None:
26:         """Start consuming messages from the queue."""
27:         self.running = True
28:         logger.info(f"Worker starting on queue: {self.queue_name}")
29:         self.channel.basic_consume(
30:             queue=self.queue_name,
31:             on_message_callback=self._on_message,
32:         )
33:         while self.running:
34:             self.channel.process_data_events(time_limit=1)
35: 
36:     def _on_message(self, ch, method, properties, body) -> None:
37:         """Handle an incoming message."""
38:         delivery_tag = method.delivery_tag
39:         logger.info(f"Received message: tag={delivery_tag}")
40: 
41:         ch.basic_ack(delivery_tag=delivery_tag)
42: 
43:         data = json.loads(body)
44:         self._process_with_retry(data, delivery_tag)
45: 
46:     def _process_with_retry(self, data: Dict, delivery_tag: int) -> None:
47:         """Process message with retry logic."""
48:         attempt = 0
49:         while attempt < self.max_retries:
50:             try:
51:                 self.handler(data)
52:                 self.stats['processed'] += 1
53:                 logger.info(f"Message processed successfully: tag={delivery_tag}")
54:                 return
55:             except Exception as e:
56:                 attempt += 1
57:                 self.stats['retried'] += 1
58:                 logger.warning(
59:                     f"Processing failed (attempt {attempt}/{self.max_retries}): {e}"
60:                 )
61:                 time.sleep(self.retry_delay)
62: 
63:         logger.error(f"Message exhausted retries, sending to DLQ: tag={delivery_tag}")
64:         self._send_to_dlq(data)
65:         self.stats['failed'] += 1
66: 
67:     def _send_to_dlq(self, data: Dict) -> None:
68:         """Send a failed message to the dead-letter queue."""
69:         self.channel.basic_publish(
70:             exchange='',
71:             routing_key=self.dead_letter_queue,
72:             body=json.dumps(data),
73:         )
74:         logger.info(f"Message sent to DLQ: {self.dead_letter_queue}")
75: 
76:     def stop(self) -> None:
77:         """Stop the worker."""
78:         self.running = False
79:         logger.info("Worker stopping...")
80: 
81:     def get_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
82:         """Return worker statistics."""
83:         return {
84:             'queue': self.queue_name,
85:             'processed': self.stats['processed'],
86:             'failed': self.stats['failed'],
87:             'retried': self.stats['retried'],
88:             'running': self.running,
89:         }

11 +class QueueWorker:
12 +    """Processes messages from a queue with retry and dead-letter."""
13 +
14 +    def __init__(self, channel, queue_name: str, handler: Callable,
15 +                 max_retries: int = 3, retry_delay: float = 5.0):
16 +        self.channel = channel
17 +        self.queue_name = queue_name
18 +        self.handler = handler
19 +        self.max_retries = max_retries
20 +        self.retry_delay = retry_delay
21 +        self.dead_letter_queue: str = f"{queue_name}.dlq"
22 +        self.running = False
23 +        self.stats = {'processed': 0, 'failed': 0, 'retried': 0}
24 +
25 +    def start(self) -> None:
26 +        """Start consuming messages from the queue."""
27 +        self.running = True
28 +        logger.info(f"Worker starting on queue: {self.queue_name}")
29 +        self.channel.basic_consume(
30 +            queue=self.queue_name,
31 +            on_message_callback=self._on_message,
32 +        )
33 +        while self.running:
34 +            self.channel.process_data_events(time_limit=1)
35 +
36 +    def _on_message(self, ch, method, properties, body) -> None:
37 +        """Handle an incoming message."""
38 +        delivery_tag = method.delivery_tag
39 +        logger.info(f"Received message: tag={delivery_tag}")
40 +
41 +        ch.basic_ack(delivery_tag=delivery_tag)
42 +
43 +        data = json.loads(body)
44 +        self._process_with_retry(data, delivery_tag)
45 +
46 +    def _process_with_retry(self, data: Dict, delivery_tag: int) -> None:
47 +        """Process message with retry logic."""
48 +        attempt = 0
49 +        while attempt < self.max_retries:
50 +            try:
51 +                self.handler(data)
52 +                self.stats['processed'] += 1
53 +                logger.info(f"Message processed successfully: tag={delivery_tag}")

64 +        self._send_to_dlq(data)
65 +        self.stats['failed'] += 1

Each failing message blocks the worker for 15 seconds of sleeping, adding 150 seconds of idle time during which 90 healthy messages sit in the queue. Should use `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` to process messages concurrently, or at minimum requeue failed messages with a delay header instead of sleeping inline.",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Blocking single-threaded retry with time.sleep() starves healthy messages; use concurrent processing."
  }
]

24: 
25:     def __init__(self, dsn: str, min_conn: int = 2, max_conn: int = 10):
26:         self.pool = pool.SimpleConnectionPool(min_conn, max_conn, dsn)
27:         logger.info(f"Database pool created: min={min_conn}, max={max_conn}")
28: 
29:     def run_query(self, sql: str, params: tuple = ()) -> List[Row]:

32:         try:
33:             logger.debug(f"Executing: {sql} with {params}")

39:                 row_dict = dict(zip(columns, row_data))
40:                 rows.append(Row(data=row_dict, columns=columns))
41:             conn.commit()

43:         except Exception as e:
44:             conn.rollback()
45:             logger.error(f"Query failed: {e}")
46:             raise

49:         """Execute a query and return a single typed Row or None."""

52:             logger.debug(f"Fetching one: {sql}")
53:             cursor = conn.cursor()

62:             raise

66:         conn = self.pool.getconn()
67:         try:
68:             cursor = conn.cursor()

15 +    """Authenticated user model."""

17 +        self.id = id
18 +        self.email = email
19 +        self.roles = roles
20 +

22 +def get_current_user(token: str) -> Optional[User]:
23 +    """Decode JWT and return a User model instance."""
24 +    try:
25 +        payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=['HS256'])

30 +        )
31 +    except jwt.InvalidTokenError:

36 +def require_auth(roles: List[str] = None):
37 +    """Decorator that requires authentication and optional role check."""
38 +    def decorator(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
39 +        @functools.wraps(fn)
40 +        def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
41 +            auth_header = request.headers.get('Authorization', '')
42 +            if not auth_header.startswith('Bearer '):
43 +                return jsonify({'error': 'Missing auth token'}), 401
44 +
45 +            token = auth_header.split(' ', 1)[1]
46 +            user = get_current_user(token)
47 +            if user is None:
48 +                return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid token'}), 401
49 +
50 +            if roles and not any(r in user.roles for r in roles):
51 +                return jsonify({'error': 'Insufficient permissions'}), 403
52 +
53 +            g.current_user = user
54 +            return fn(*args, **kwargs)
55 +        return wrapper
56 +    return decorator

59 +def require_api_key(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
60 +    """Decorator that requires a valid API key in headers."""
61 +    @functools.wraps(fn)

63 +        api_key = request.headers.get('X-API-Key')
64 +        if not api_key:
65 +            return jsonify({'error': 'API key required'}), 401
66 +        g.api_key = api_key
67 +        return fn(*args, **kwargs)
68 +    return wrapper
69 +
70 +
71 +def extract_token_claims(token: str) -> dict:
72 +    """Extract claims from JWT without full validation."""

74 +        return jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=['HS256'])
75 +    except jwt.InvalidTokenError:

3: import os
4: import json
5: import logging
6: from typing import Any, Optional, Dict, List
7: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
8: 
9: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
10: 
11: 
12: @dataclass
13: class FeatureFlags:
14:     """Typed feature flag container."""
15:     enable_v2: bool = False
16:     dark_mode: bool = False
17:     max_upload_size: int = 10
18:     allowed_origins: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
19: 
20: 
21: class Config:
22:     """Centralized configuration loaded from file with env overrides."""
23: 
24:     _instance: Optional['Config'] = None
25:     _config_data: Dict[str, Any] = {}
26: 
27:     def __init__(self, config_path: str = 'config.json'):
28:         self._load_config(config_path)
29: 
30:     def _load_config(self, config_path: str) -> None:
31:         """Load configuration from JSON file."""
32:         logger.info(f"Loading config from {config_path}")
33:         raw = open(config_path).read()
34:         self._config_data = json.loads(raw)
35:         logger.info(f"Loaded {len(self._config_data)} config keys")
36: 
37:     @classmethod
38:     def get_instance(cls, config_path: str = 'config.json') -> 'Config':
39:         """Get or create singleton instance."""
40:         if cls._instance is None:
41:             cls._instance = Config(config_path)
42:         return cls._instance
43: 
44:     def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
45:         """Get a config value with env var override."""
46:         env_key = key.upper()
47:         value = os.environ.get(env_key) or self._config_data.get(key, default)
48:         return value
49: 
50:     def get_required(self, key: str) -> Any:
51:         """Get a config value, raising if not found."""
52:         value = self.get(key)
53:         if value is None:
54:             raise ValueError(f"Required config key '{key}' not found")
55:         return value
56: 
57:     def get_feature_flags(self) -> FeatureFlags:
58:         """Return typed feature flags from config."""
59:         flags_data = self._config_data.get('feature_flags', {})
60:         return FeatureFlags(
61:             enable_v2=flags_data.get('enable_v2', False),
62:             dark_mode=flags_data.get('dark_mode', False),
63:             max_upload_size=flags_data.get('max_upload_size', 10),
64:             allowed_origins=flags_data.get('allowed_origins', []),
65:         )
66: 
67:     def get_database_config(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
68:         """Return database configuration."""
69:         return {
70:             'url': self.get('database_url'),
71:             'pool_size': self.get('database_pool_size', 5),
72:             'timeout': self.get('database_timeout', 30),
73:         }
74: 
75:     def get_all(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
76:         """Return a copy of all config data."""
77:         return dict(self._config_data)
78: 
79:     def reload(self, config_path: str = 'config.json') -> None:
80:         """Reload configuration from file."""
81:         logger.info("Reloading configuration...")
82:         self._load_config(config_path)
83: 
84:     def set(self, key: str, value: Any) -> None:
85:         """Set a config value at runtime."""
86:         self._config_data[key] = value
87:         logger.info(f"Config key '{key}' updated")
88: 
89:     @classmethod
90:     def reset(cls) -> None:
91:         """Reset singleton for testing."""
92:         cls._instance = None
93:         cls._config_data = {}

21 +class Config:

33: class EventEmitter:

38:         self._global_listeners: List[Callable] = []

69:         BigDecimal currentBalance = orderRepository.getBalance(accountId);
70:         if (currentBalance.compareTo(amount) >= 0) {
71:             BigDecimal newBalance = currentBalance.subtract(amount);
72:             orderRepository.setBalance(accountId, newBalance);
73:             logger.info("Deducted {} from account {}, new balance: {}", amount, accountId, newBalance);

32:     public AuthResult authenticate(String username, String password) {
33:         logger.info("Login attempt for user: " + username + " with password: " + password);
34: 
35:         if (isLockedOut(username)) {
36:             logger.warn("Account locked for user: {}", username);
37:             return AuthResult.locked();
38:         }
39: 
40:         User user = userRepository.findByUsername(username);
41:         if (user == null) {
42:             incrementAttempts(username);
43:             return AuthResult.failure("Invalid credentials");
44:         }
45: 
46:         String storedHash = user.getPasswordHash();
47:         String inputHash = hashPassword(password, user.getSalt());
48: 
49:         if (inputHash.equals(storedHash)) {
50:             loginAttempts.remove(username);
51:             String sessionId = UUID.randomUUID().toString();
52:             sessionStore.create(sessionId, user.getId(), Instant.now().plusSeconds(3600));
53:             logger.info("User {} authenticated successfully, session: {}", username, sessionId);
54:             return AuthResult.success(sessionId);
55:         } else {
56:             incrementAttempts(username);
57:             return AuthResult.failure("Invalid credentials");
58:         }
59:     }
60: 
61:     private void incrementAttempts(String username) {
62:         int current = loginAttempts.getOrDefault(username, 0);
63:         loginAttempts.put(username, current + 1);
64:         if (current + 1 >= MAX_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS) {
65:             lockoutTimestamps.put(username, System.currentTimeMillis());
66:             logger.warn("Account {} locked after {} failed attempts", username, current + 1);
67:         }
68:     }
69: 
70:     private boolean isLockedOut(String username) {
71:         Long lockedAt = lockoutTimestamps.get(username);
72:         if (lockedAt == null) return false;
73:         if (System.currentTimeMillis() - lockedAt > LOCKOUT_DURATION_MS) {
74:             lockoutTimestamps.remove(username);
75:             loginAttempts.remove(username);
76:             return false;
77:         }
78:         return true;
79:     }
80: 
81:     private String hashPassword(String password, String salt) {
82:         try {
83:             MessageDigest md = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256");
84:             md.update(salt.getBytes());
85:             byte[] hash = md.digest(password.getBytes());
86:             StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
87:             for (byte b : hash) {
88:                 sb.append(String.format("%02x", b));
89:             }
90:             return sb.toString();
91:         } catch (Exception e) {
92:             throw new RuntimeException("Hashing failed", e);
93:         }
94:     }

3: import java.util.HashMap;
4: import java.util.Map;
5: import java.util.Iterator;
6: import org.slf4j.Logger;
7: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
8: 
9: public class CacheManagerService {
10: 
11:     private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CacheManagerService.class);
12:     private static final long DEFAULT_TTL_MS = 300_000;
13: 
14:     private final Map<String, CacheEntry> cache = new HashMap<>();
15:     private final int maxSize;
16: 
17:     public CacheManagerService(int maxSize) {
18:         this.maxSize = maxSize;
19:     }
20: 
21:     public synchronized Object get(String key) {
22:         CacheEntry entry = cache.get(key);
23:         if (entry == null) {
24:             logger.debug("Cache miss for key: {}", key);
25:             return null;
26:         }
27:         if (System.currentTimeMillis() <= entry.getExpiresAt()) {
28:             logger.debug("Cache hit for key: {}", key);
29:             return entry.getValue();
30:         }
31:         cache.remove(key);
32:         logger.debug("Cache entry expired for key: {}", key);
33:         return null;
34:     }
35: 
36:     public void put(String key, Object value) {
37:         put(key, value, DEFAULT_TTL_MS);
38:     }
39: 
40:     public void put(String key, Object value, long ttlMs) {
41:         if (cache.size() >= maxSize) {
42:             evictExpired();
43:         }
44:         if (cache.size() >= maxSize) {
45:             String oldestKey = cache.keySet().iterator().next();
46:             cache.remove(oldestKey);
47:             logger.info("Evicted oldest entry: {}", oldestKey);
48:         }
49:         long expiresAt = System.currentTimeMillis() + ttlMs;
50:         cache.put(key, new CacheEntry(value, expiresAt));
51:     }
52: 
53:     public void invalidate(String key) {
54:         cache.remove(key);
55:     }
56: 
57:     public void clear() {
58:         cache.clear();
59:         logger.info("Cache cleared");
60:     }
61: 
62:     private void evictExpired() {
63:         long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
64:         for (Map.Entry<String, CacheEntry> entry : cache.entrySet()) {
65:             if (now > entry.getValue().getExpiresAt()) {
66:                 cache.remove(entry.getKey());
67:                 logger.debug("Evicted expired entry: {}", entry.getKey());
68:             }
69:         }
70:     }
71: 
72:     public int size() {
73:         return cache.size();
74:     }
75: 
76:     private static class CacheEntry {
77:         private final Object value;
78:         private final long expiresAt;
79: 
80:         CacheEntry(Object value, long expiresAt) {
81:             this.value = value;
82:             this.expiresAt = expiresAt;
83:         }
84: 
85:         Object getValue() { return value; }
86:         long getExpiresAt() { return expiresAt; }
87:     }

40:     public void sendNotification(String userId, String templateName, Map<String, String> params) {
41:         User user = userRepository.findById(userId);
42:         String channel = user.getPreferences().getChannel();
43: 
44:         if (!rateLimiter.tryAcquire(userId)) {
45:             logger.warn("Rate limit exceeded for user: {}", userId);
46:             return;
47:         }
48: 
49:         String template = templateEngine.load(templateName);
50:         String body = template;
51:         for (Map.Entry<String, String> entry : params.entrySet()) {
52:             body = body.replace("{ {" + entry.getKey() + "} }", entry.getValue());
53:         }
54: 
55:         dispatchToChannel(channel, user, body);
56:     }
57: 
58:     private void dispatchToChannel(String channel, User user, String body) {
59:         ExecutorService executor = Executors.newSingleThreadExecutor();
60:         executor.submit(() -> {
61:             try {
62:                 switch (channel) {
63:                     case "email":
64:                         emailClient.send(user.getEmail(), "Notification", body);
65:                         break;
66:                     case "sms":
67:                         smsClient.send(user.getPhoneNumber(), body);
68:                         break;
69:                     case "push":
70:                         pushClient.send(user.getDeviceToken(), body);
71:                         break;
72:                     default:
73:                         logger.warn("Unknown channel: {}", channel);
74:                 }
75:                 logger.info("Notification sent to user {} via {}", user.getId(), channel);
76:             } catch (Exception e) {
77:                 /* retry later */
78:             }
79:         });
80:     }

82:     public void sendBulkNotification(String templateName, Map<String, String> params, String channel) {
83:         List<User> users = userRepository.findAll();
84:         for (User user : users) {
85:             try {
86:                 String template = templateEngine.load(templateName);
87:                 String body = template;
88:                 for (Map.Entry<String, String> entry : params.entrySet()) {
89:                     body = body.replace("{ {" + entry.getKey() + "} }", entry.getValue());
90:                 }
91:                 body = body.replace("{ {name} }", user.getName());
92:                 dispatchToChannel(channel, user, body);
93:             } catch (Exception e) {

95:             }
96:         }

59:             int stock = db.getStockLevel(itemId);

32:     public Report generateReport(String startDate, String endDate, String reportType) {
33:         logger.info("Generating report for period: " + startDate + " to " + endDate);
34: 
35:         Date parsedStart = null;
36:         Date parsedEnd = null;
37:         try {
38:             parsedStart = sdf.parse(startDate);
39:             parsedEnd = sdf.parse(endDate);
40:         } catch (Exception e) {
41:             throw new RuntimeException("Invalid date format", e);
42:         }
43: 
44:         LocalDate start = LocalDate.parse(startDate);
45:         LocalDate end = LocalDate.parse(endDate);
46: 
47:         List<Record> allRecords = new ArrayList<>();
48:         for (LocalDate d = start; d.isBefore(end); d = d.plusDays(1)) {
49:             List<Record> daily = recordRepo.findByDate(d);
50:             allRecords.addAll(daily);
51:         }
52: 
53:         double totalRevenue = 0.0;
54:         double totalCost = 0.0;
55:         for (Record r : allRecords) {
56:             totalRevenue += r.getRevenue();
57:             totalCost += r.getCost();
58:         }
59: 
60:         String csv = "";
61:         for (Record r : allRecords) {
62:             csv += r.toCsv() + "\n";
63:         }
64: 
65:         Report report = new Report();
66:         report.setStartDate(startDate);
67:         report.setEndDate(endDate);
68:         report.setType(reportType);
69:         report.setTotalRevenue(totalRevenue);
70:         report.setTotalCost(totalCost);
71:         report.setProfit(totalRevenue - totalCost);
72:         report.setCsvData(csv);
73: 
74:         byte[] pdf = pdfGenerator.generate(report);
75:         report.setPdfData(pdf);
76: 
77:         reportRepo.save(report);
78:         logger.info("Report generated successfully with {} records", allRecords.size());
79:         return report;
80:     }

10: @Service
11: public class EventPublisherService {
12: 
13:     private static final int MAX_RETRIES = 5;
14:     private static final long RETRY_DELAY_MS = 1000;
15:     private final KafkaTemplate<String, String> kafkaTemplate;
16:     private final ObjectMapper objectMapper;
17:     private final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(EventPublisherService.class);
18: 
19:     public EventPublisherService(KafkaTemplate<String, String> kafkaTemplate, ObjectMapper objectMapper) {
20:         this.kafkaTemplate = kafkaTemplate;
21:         this.objectMapper = objectMapper;
22:     }
<- CUT CONTENT ->
30:     public void publishEvent(String topic, DomainEvent event) {
31:         int retries = 0;
32:         while (retries < MAX_RETRIES) {
33:             try {
34:                 String payload = objectMapper.writeValueAsString(event);
35:                 kafkaTemplate.send(topic, event.getAggregateId(), payload);
36:                 logger.info("Published event {} to topic {}", event.getEventType(), topic);
37:                 return;
38:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
39:                 logger.warn("Publish interrupted, retrying...");
40:                 // retry
41:             } catch (Exception e) {
42:                 logger.error("Failed to publish event: {}", e.getMessage());
43:                 retries++;
44:                 try {
45:                     TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS.sleep(RETRY_DELAY_MS);
46:                 } catch (InterruptedException ie) {
47:                     // ignore
48:                 }
49:             }
50:         }
51:         logger.error("Exhausted retries for event {} on topic {}", event.getEventType(), topic);
52:     }
53: 
54:     public DomainEvent deserializeEvent(byte[] payload) {
55:         try {
56:             ObjectInputStream ois = new ObjectInputStream(new ByteArrayInputStream(payload));
57:             DomainEvent event = (DomainEvent) ois.readObject();
58:             ois.close();
59:             return event;
60:         } catch (Exception e) {
61:             logger.error("Failed to deserialize event: {}", e.getMessage());
62:             throw new RuntimeException("Deserialization failed", e);
63:         }
64:     }
65: 
66:     public void publishBatch(String topic, List<DomainEvent> events) {
67:         for (DomainEvent event : events) {
68:             publishEvent(topic, event);
69:         }
70:         logger.info("Batch of {} events published to {}", events.size(), topic);
71:     }
72: 
73:     public void publishWithKey(String topic, String key, DomainEvent event) {
74:         try {
75:             String payload = objectMapper.writeValueAsString(event);
76:             kafkaTemplate.send(topic, key, payload);
77:             logger.info("Published keyed event to {}: key={}", topic, key);
78:         } catch (Exception e) {
79:             logger.error("Failed to publish keyed event", e);
80:             throw new RuntimeException(e);
81:         }
82:     }
83: }

The `readObject()` call triggers arbitrary code during deserialization before any type check. Concrete scenario: attacker sends a payload containing an Apache Commons Collections `InvokerTransformer` chain that executes `Runtime.exec(\"rm -rf /\")` when deserialized. Replace Java serialization with JSON deserialization using the existing `objectMapper`.",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Insecure deserialization -- ObjectInputStream.readObject on untrusted payload enables Remote Code Execution via gadget chains"
  }

4: import java.util.List;
5: import java.util.ArrayList;
6: import javax.persistence.EntityManager;
7: import javax.persistence.PersistenceContext;
8: import org.slf4j.Logger;
9: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
10: 
11: @Service
12: public class DataMigrationService {
13: 
14:     @PersistenceContext
15:     private EntityManager entityManager;
16:     private final UserRepository userRepo;
17:     private final LegacyDatabaseClient legacyDb;
18:     private final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(DataMigrationService.class);
19:     private static final int BATCH_SIZE = 500;
20: 
21:     public DataMigrationService(UserRepository userRepo, LegacyDatabaseClient legacyDb) {
22:         this.userRepo = userRepo;
23:         this.legacyDb = legacyDb;
24:     }
<- CUT CONTENT ->
32:     public MigrationResult migrateUsers() {
33:         List<LegacyUser> allUsers = legacyDb.fetchAllUsers();
34:         int totalMigrated = 0;
35:         int totalFailed = 0;
36:         List<String> errors = new ArrayList<>();
37: 
38:         for (int batchStart = 0; batchStart < allUsers.size(); batchStart += BATCH_SIZE) {
39:             int batchEnd = Math.min(batchStart + BATCH_SIZE, allUsers.size());
40:             List<LegacyUser> batch = allUsers.subList(batchStart, batchEnd);
41: 
42:             for (LegacyUser legacyUser : batch) {
43:                 try {
44:                     logger.debug("Migrating user: " + legacyUser.getSsn() + " email: " + legacyUser.getEmail());
45: 
46:                     NewUser newUser = convertUser(legacyUser);
47: 
48:                     if (legacyUser.getBalance().equals(newUser.getBalance())) {
49:                         newUser.setBalanceVerified(true);
50:                     } else {
51:                         newUser.setBalanceVerified(false);
52:                         logger.warn("Balance mismatch for user {}", legacyUser.getId());
53:                     }
54: 
55:                     entityManager.persist(newUser);
56:                     entityManager.flush();
57:                     entityManager.clear();
58: 
59:                     totalMigrated++;
60:                 } catch (Exception e) {
61:                     totalFailed++;
62:                     errors.add("Failed user " + legacyUser.getId() + ": " + e.getMessage());
63:                     logger.error("Migration failed for user {}", legacyUser.getId(), e);
64:                 }
65:             }
66: 
67:             logger.info("Batch {}-{} complete. Migrated: {}, Failed: {}",
68:                 batchStart, batchEnd, totalMigrated, totalFailed);
69:         }
70: 
71:         return new MigrationResult(totalMigrated, totalFailed, errors);
72:     }
73: 
74:     private NewUser convertUser(LegacyUser legacy) {
75:         NewUser user = new NewUser();
76:         user.setId(legacy.getId());
77:         user.setFullName(legacy.getFirstName() + " " + legacy.getLastName());
78:         user.setEmail(legacy.getEmail());
79:         user.setBalance(legacy.getBalance());
80:         user.setCreatedAt(legacy.getRegistrationDate());
81:         user.setActive(legacy.getStatus().equals("ACTIVE"));
82:         return user;
83:     }
84: 
85:     public void rollbackMigration(List<String> userIds) {
86:         for (String userId : userIds) {
87:             try {
88:                 userRepo.deleteById(userId);
89:             } catch (Exception e) {
90:                 logger.error("Failed to rollback user {}", userId, e);
91:             }
92:         }
93:         logger.info("Rollback completed for {} users", userIds.size());
94:     }
95: 
96:     public long countMigratedUsers() {
97:         return userRepo.count();
98:     }
99: 
100:     public boolean isMigrationComplete() {
101:         long legacyCount = legacyDb.countUsers();
102:         long newCount = userRepo.count();
103:         return newCount >= legacyCount;
104:     }
105: }

4: import java.util.Map;
5: import java.util.concurrent.*;
6: import java.util.HashMap;
7: import org.slf4j.Logger;
8: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
9: 
10: @Component
11: public class ScheduledTaskExecutor {
12: 
13:     private final ScheduledExecutorService executorService;
14:     private final Map<String, ScheduledFuture<?>> activeTasks;
15:     private final TaskRepository taskRepo;
16:     private final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ScheduledTaskExecutor.class);
17: 
18:     public ScheduledTaskExecutor(TaskRepository taskRepo) {
19:         this.taskRepo = taskRepo;
20:         this.executorService = Executors.newScheduledThreadPool(10);
21:         this.activeTasks = new HashMap<>();
22:     }
<- CUT CONTENT ->
30:     public void scheduleTask(String taskId, Runnable task, long intervalMs) {
31:         Runnable wrappedTask = new Runnable() {
32:             @Override
33:             public void run() {
34:                 synchronized (ScheduledTaskExecutor.this) {
35:                     try {
36:                         logger.info("Executing task: {}", taskId);
37:                         task.run();
38:                         taskRepo.updateLastRun(taskId, System.currentTimeMillis());
39:                         logger.info("Task {} completed successfully", taskId);
40:                     } catch (Exception e) {
41:                         logger.error("Task {} failed: {}", taskId, e.getMessage());
42:                         taskRepo.recordFailure(taskId, e.getMessage());
43:                     }
44:                 }
45:             }
46:         };
47: 
48:         ScheduledFuture<?> future = executorService.scheduleAtFixedRate(
49:             wrappedTask, 0, intervalMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS
50:         );
51:         activeTasks.put(taskId, future);
52:         logger.info("Scheduled task {} with interval {}ms", taskId, intervalMs);
53:     }
54: 
55:     public void cancelTask(String taskId) {
56:         ScheduledFuture<?> future = activeTasks.remove(taskId);
57:         if (future != null) {
58:             future.cancel(false);
59:             logger.info("Cancelled task: {}", taskId);
60:         }
61:     }
62: 
63:     public Map<String, TaskStatus> getHealthStatus() {
64:         Map<String, TaskStatus> status = new HashMap<>();
65:         for (Map.Entry<String, ScheduledFuture<?>> entry : activeTasks.entrySet()) {
66:             String taskId = entry.getKey();
67:             ScheduledFuture<?> future = entry.getValue();
68:             boolean running = !future.isDone() && !future.isCancelled();
69:             long lastRun = taskRepo.getLastRun(taskId);
70:             status.put(taskId, new TaskStatus(taskId, running, lastRun));
71:         }
72:         return status;
73:     }
74: 
75:     public int getActiveTaskCount() {
76:         return activeTasks.size();
77:     }
78: 
79:     public void cancelAllTasks() {
80:         for (ScheduledFuture<?> future : activeTasks.values()) {
81:             future.cancel(true);
82:         }
83:         activeTasks.clear();
84:         logger.info("All tasks cancelled");
85:     }
86: 
87:     public List<String> getTaskIds() {
88:         return new java.util.ArrayList<>(activeTasks.keySet());
89:     }
90: }
vars.prSummary: Add scheduled task executor with cron-like scheduling, concurrency control, health monitoring, and task cancellation.

3: require 'stripe'
4: require 'bigdecimal'
5: 
6: class PaymentProcessor
7:   class PaymentError < StandardError; end
8: 
9:   def initialize(stripe_client, db, logger)
10:     @stripe = stripe_client
11:     @db = db
12:     @logger = logger
13:   end
14: 
15:   def process_payment(order_id, amount, currency = 'usd')
16:     if amount
17:       @logger.info("Processing payment of #{amount} #{currency}")
18:     else
19:       @logger.warn("No amount provided, skipping")
20:       return { success: false, error: 'no_amount' }
21:     end
22: 
23:     order = find_order(order_id)
24:     return { success: false, error: 'order_not_found' } unless order
25: 
26:     charge = @stripe.charges.create(
27:       amount: (amount * 100).to_i,
28:       currency: currency,
29:       source: order.payment_source
30:     )
31: 
32:     record_transaction(order_id, charge.id, amount)
33:     send_receipt(order)
34:     { success: true, charge_id: charge.id }
35:   rescue Stripe::CardError => e
36:     @logger.error("Card error: #{e.message}")
37:     { success: false, error: e.message }
38:   end
39: 
40:   def find_order(order_id)
41:     @db.execute("SELECT * FROM orders WHERE id = '#{order_id}'")
42:   end
43: 
44:   def refund_payment(charge_id, reason = nil)
45:     refund = @stripe.refunds.create(charge: charge_id, reason: reason)
46:     @logger.info("Refund issued: #{refund.id}")
47:     refund
48:   end
49: 
50:   def calculate_order_total(order_id)
51:     orders = Order.where(parent_id: order_id)
52:     total = 0.0
53:     orders.each do |o|
54:       o.line_items.each do |li|
55:         total += li.price * li.quantity
56:       end
57:     end
58:     total
59:   end
60: 
61:   def batch_process(order_ids)
62:     results = []
63:     order_ids.each do |oid|
64:       order = Order.find(oid)
65:       total = calculate_order_total(oid)
66:       results << process_payment(oid, total)
67:     end
68:     results
69:   end
70: 
71:   private
72: 
73:   def record_transaction(order_id, charge_id, amount)
74:     @db.execute(
75:       "INSERT INTO transactions (order_id, charge_id, amount) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
76:       [order_id, charge_id, amount]
77:     )
78:   end
79: 
80:   def send_receipt(order)
81:     user = User.where("email = '#{order.user_email}'")
82:     return unless user
83: 
84:     ReceiptMailer.send(
85:       to: order.user_email,
86:       subject: 'Payment Receipt',
87:       body: receipt_body(order)
88:     )
89:   end
90: 
91:   def receipt_body(order)
92:     items = order.line_items.map { |li| "#{li.name}: $#{li.price}" }
93:     items.join("\n")
94:   end
95: end
